languages.notes: Rudrabha/Wav2Lip README: works for any identity, voice, and language. Audio-driven — not a finite language pack.

license.summary: Open code/weights restricted to personal, academic, or non-commercial use (trained on LRS2).
caveats: Commercial use of the open models is strictly prohibited without a separate commercial arrangement (Sync Labs / authors).
Do not treat community forks’ MIT/Apache claims as covering the original Rudrabha/Wav2Lip weights.

languages.notes: Official MuseTalk README lists audio support for Chinese, English, and Japanese.

license.summary: Code and trained MuseTalk models are MIT / free for academic and commercial use per project disclaimer.
caveats: Third-party deps (e.g. Whisper, DWPose, VAE) keep their own licenses — comply with those as well.
Sample test data in the repo is for non-commercial research only.

languages.notes: Audio→3DMM pipeline; no closed language list published. Lip motion follows the input audio track; multilingual speech is commonly used in demos but not enumerated by the authors.

license.summary: Apache-2.0 (non-commercial restriction removed). Attribution and Apache terms still apply.
caveats: Third-party components (GFPGAN/basicsr/etc.) may add their own terms.
Tencent trademark/name restrictions in the project disclaimer still apply.
Bundled Wav2Lip checkpoint inherits Wav2Lip’s non-commercial terms if you use that path.

languages.notes: Portrait + audio img2vid; no official closed language list. Quality depends on the input audio, not a language pack.

license.summary: Creative Commons BY-NC-SA 4.0 — non-commercial; ShareAlike on adaptations.
caveats: Authors explicitly point commercial users to Tencent Cloud VCLM instead of this open release.
ShareAlike can complicate proprietary product packaging.

languages.notes: LivePortrait itself is a general motion/warp stack. Our bench path uses JoyVASA for audio→motion; JoyVASA documents training on private Chinese + public English data (multilingual claim centered on those).

license.summary: LivePortrait (KwaiVGI) and JoyVASA are MIT-licensed for code; permissive for commercial use with attribution.
caveats: Confirm licenses for every checkpoint you download (InsightFace / detection weights may differ).
MIT still requires retaining copyright notices.

languages.notes: GAIR-NLP/LiveTalk publishes no official language pack. Audio path uses facebook/wav2vec2-base-960h (English ASR-pretrained); cross-lingual speech is not a documented guarantee.

license.summary: Project README states Apache-2.0 release (GitHub license metadata may show NOASSERTION — verify LICENSE file in your clone).
caveats: Depends on Wan2.1 / OmniAvatar stacks — verify those component licenses for your deployment.
Apache-2.0 requires NOTICE/attribution compliance.

languages.notes: EchoMimicV3 project page shows Chinese- and English-driven audio demos. Flash path uses chinese-wav2vec2-base; preview path uses wav2vec2-base-960h. Not marketed as a larger closed multilingual pack beyond these.

license.summary: EchoMimicV3 models are Apache-2.0; authors claim no rights over your generated content subject to license + law.
caveats: Base Wan2.1-Fun weights and other deps keep their own terms.
Responsible-use / prohibited-content clauses in the project license still apply.

languages.notes: Audio-driven (HuBERT). No official closed language list from Ant Group README.

caveats: TensorRT / ONNX auxiliary models may carry additional terms — check checkpoint cards.
Apache-2.0 attribution required.

languages.notes: No official language pack. Streaming audio encoder is facebook/wav2vec2-base-960h (English-pretrained). Cross-lingual audio may work in practice but is not a documented guarantee.

license.summary: SoulX-FlashHead code is Apache-2.0; maintainers confirmed commercial use is allowed if you follow applicable law and license terms.
caveats: Retain Apache notices; do not imply SoulApp/Soul-AILab endorsement.
Authors disclaim legal responsibility for downstream misuse; comply with local law (including China if applicable to your use).

languages.notes: Same family as Lite: no official language pack; uses the same wav2vec2-base-960h audio encoder stack.

license.summary: Same SoulX-FlashHead Apache-2.0 release as Lite (Model_Pro weights in the same HF package).
caveats: Same attribution and lawful-use requirements as Lite.
Heavier Wan VAE path — still under the project Apache-2.0 terms.
